title: Uragoga carthagenensis
taxonomy: Uragoga carthagenensis is classified in the family Rubiaceae, a large family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. It is a genus of flowering plants that belongs to the order Gentianales. The Rubiaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants, with over 600 genera and 13,000 species. Uragoga carthagenensis is a species of plant native to tropical regions, specifically found in the Caribbean and Central America. intro: Uragoga carthagenensis, also known as West Indian asafoetida or Giant hogweed, is a plant species belonging to the family Apiaceae, native to South America. The health benefits of Uragoga carthagenensis include its potential use in treating digestive issues, fever reduction, and as an anti-inflammatory agent, due to its rich content of volatile oils and other bioactive compounds. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Uragoga carthagenensis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in the preparation of poultices and ointments to treat various health conditions.
